an2.62:1.1: “Monks, I will teach you the communal living of the bad & the communal living of the good.
an2.62:1.2: Listen to that & pay careful attention. I will speak.”
an2.62:1.3: “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to the Blessed One.
an2.62:2.1: “And how is there the communal living of the bad? And how do the bad live together?
an2.62:2.2: “There is the case where the thought occurs to an elder monk,
an2.62:2.4: ‘I should not correct an elder monk, nor should I correct a monk of middling standing, nor should I correct a newcoming monk.
an2.62:2.5: “‘If an elder monk were to correct me, he would correct me unsympathetic for my welfare, and not sympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “No,” and that would annoy him. Even seeing (my offense), I wouldn’t make amends.
an2.62:2.6: “‘If a monk of middle standing … a newcoming monk were to correct me, he would correct me unsympathetic for my welfare, and not sympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “No,” and that would annoy him. Even seeing (my offense), I wouldn’t make amends.
an2.62:2.7: “The thought occurs to a monk of middle standing… a newcoming monk,
an2.62:2.9: ‘I should not correct an elder monk, nor should I correct a monk of middling standing, nor should I correct a newcoming monk.
an2.62:2.10: “‘If an elder monk were to correct me, he would correct me unsympathetic for my welfare, and not sympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “No,” and that would annoy him. Even seeing (my offense), I wouldn’t make amends.
an2.62:2.11: “‘If a monk of middle standing … a newcoming monk were to correct me, he would correct me unsympathetic for my welfare, and not sympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “No,” and that would annoy him. Even seeing (my offense), I wouldn’t make amends.
an2.62:3.1: “And how is there the communal living of the good? And how do the good live together?
an2.62:3.2: “There is the case where the thought occurs to an elder monk,
an2.62:3.4: ‘I should correct an elder monk, I should correct a monk of middling standing, and I should correct a newcoming monk.
an2.62:3.5: “‘If an elder monk were to correct me, he would correct me sympathetic for my welfare, and not unsympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “Good,” and that would not annoy him. Seeing (my offense), I would make amends.
an2.62:3.6: “‘If a monk of middle standing … a newcoming monk were to correct me, he would correct me sympathetic for my welfare, and not unsympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “Good,” and that would not annoy him. Seeing (my offense), I would make amends.
an2.62:3.7: “The thought occurs to a monk of middle standing… a newcoming monk,
an2.62:3.9: ‘I should correct an elder monk, I should correct a monk of middling standing, and I should correct a newcoming monk.
an2.62:3.10: “‘If an elder monk were to correct me, he would correct me sympathetic for my welfare, and not unsympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “Good,” and that would not annoy him. Seeing (my offense), I would make amends.
an2.62:3.11: “‘If a monk of middle standing … a newcoming monk were to correct me, he would correct me sympathetic for my welfare, and not unsympathetic for my welfare. I would say, “Good,” and that would not annoy him. Seeing (my offense), I would make amends.
an2.62:3.12: “This, monks, is how there is the communal living of the good, and this is how the good live together.”
